title: Crataegus scabrifolia
intro: Crataegus scabrifolia, also known as the scrub hawthorn, is a species of hawthorn tree native to the southeastern United States. The scrub hawthorn has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the scrub hawthorn is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote cardiovascular health, lower blood pressure, and improve overall well-being.
taxonomy: Crataegus scabrifolia is classified as a species of hawthorn, a flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Crataegus, which includes approximately 200-300 species of hawthorn trees and shrubs. Crataegus scabrifolia is native to China and is also found in certain parts of Asia. The plant is a member of the subgenus Mespilus, which is a part of the Rosaceae family. Crataegus scabrifolia's classification is further divided into different varieties and cultivars, depending on its specific characteristics and growth patterns.
